fixture.description: A bulk operation flushes the entire cache on every iteration of the loop. On large inputs this is an O(n) full-cache-invalidation storm; the flush should happen once after the loop.

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that bulkUpdate calls cache.flushAll() (a full cache invalidation) inside the per-item loop, so the whole cache is flushed N times for N items — a cache-invalidation storm / write amplification on large inputs — and recommends flushing once after the loop instead. Severity IMPORTANT, DEBT, or SUGGESTED. FAIL if the per-iteration full-flush performance problem is not surfaced. Do NOT require the words 'N+1'.

expected_behavior: Performance agent flags the full cache flush inside the loop as O(n) invalidation
Recommends moving flushAll() to after the loop
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT claim a single flushAll() call is itself wrong
Does NOT require the loop body to perform a database read to flag it
